description: PrismatAI V1 feature schema — 116 competitive units, Option 2 (type-count + instance flags + supply + card-set indicator)
player_convention: P0 = Player_One (game index 0, white). P1 = Player_Two (game index 1, black). Active player stored as input feature, NOT used to rotate P0/P1 slots. Absolute player numbering.
value_target: P(Player_0_wins) — probability that P0 wins, regardless of whose turn it is.

features.name: p0_ready
features.description: P0 count of this unit type in ready state (can use ability, not constructing, not assigned)

features.name: p0_exhausted
features.description: P0 count of this unit type exhausted (ability used or cannot use ability, not constructing, not assigned)

features.name: p0_constructing
features.description: P0 count of this unit type under construction

features.name: p0_blocking
features.description: P0 count of this unit type assigned as blocker

features.name: p1_ready
features.description: P1 count of this unit type in ready state

features.name: p1_exhausted
features.description: P1 count of this unit type exhausted

features.name: p1_constructing
features.description: P1 count of this unit type under construction

features.name: p1_blocking
features.description: P1 count of this unit type assigned as blocker

features.name: p0_supply
features.description: P0 remaining supply of this unit type (raw count)

features.name: p1_supply
features.description: P1 remaining supply of this unit type (raw count)

features.name: in_card_set
features.description: 1.0 if this unit type is buyable in this game, 0.0 otherwise

status_classification.blocking: assigned as blocker (CardStatus::Assigned in C++, role=assigned && blocking in JS)
status_classification.ready: can use ability, not constructing, not assigned (canUseAbility() in C++)
status_classification.exhausted: everything else (ability used, cannot use ability, not constructing, not blocking)

global_features.name: turn_number
global_features.description: Current ply index (per player-turn). Input feature only — distinct from ply_index used in label computation.

global_features.name: active_player
global_features.description: Whose turn it is: 0 = P0, 1 = P1. Raw binary, no normalization.

normalization_caps._note: Initial estimates from WillScore resource values and gameplay patterns. Phase 0d audit will compute actual percentile distributions and adjust so <1% of values are clipped.

B.name: temporal_sample_weighting
B.description: Hard 0/1 labels with sample_weight based on ply_index progress

C.name: elo_interpolated
C.description: Blend outcome_p0 with Elo-based p0_win_prior, decaying over ply_index

D.name: neutral_prior
D.description: Blend outcome_p0 with 0.5 prior, decaying over ply_index

label_strategies._note: Selection deferred to Phase 3 ablation results

loss.description: Model outputs raw logit z. Loss = BCE(sigmoid(z), y) where y in [0,1]. Numerically stable via WithLogits variant.
loss.inference_path: Path A: logit output -> C++ applies 2*sigmoid(z)-1 for [-1,1] range

record_metadata.total_plies: uint16 — total player-turns in game. Never an input feature.
record_metadata.rating_p0: uint16 — P0 rating at game time
record_metadata.rating_p1: uint16 — P1 rating at game time
record_metadata.card_set: uint8[11] — indices of up to 11 random units in this game (255 = empty slot)
record_metadata._note: Metadata stored separately from feature vector. Extraction test must verify no metadata field appears in feature vector indices.

mirror_augmentation.description: NOT applied by default. Phase 3 ablation tests turn-gated mirroring (only after ply N where starting Drone asymmetry <10% of mean economy).
mirror_augmentation.p0_block_indices: 0 to num_units*11-1, offsets 0-3 and 8 per unit
mirror_augmentation.p1_block_indices: 0 to num_units*11-1, offsets 4-7 and 9 per unit
mirror_augmentation.swap_operation: For each unit: swap offsets [0,1,2,3] with [4,5,6,7], swap offset 8 with 9. Flip active_player. Invert label.

compatibility.cpp_inference: NeuralNet.cpp reads num_units and state_dim from weight file header. No C++ code changes needed for 116-unit schema — C++ adapts dynamically.
compatibility.cpp_activation_change: Path A requires replacing tanhf(rawValue) with 2.0f*sigmoidf(rawValue)-1.0f in NeuralNet.cpp value head output.
compatibility.weight_format: PNET header (magic=0x504E4554, version, state_dim, num_units, hidden_dim, num_layers, num_tensors, num_unit_names) + named tensors + unit index
